questions.text: I. Ms. Kulsoom labelled Mr. Waleed a deserter.II. Ms. Kulsoom offered an apology.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
Both statements I and II are independent causes
Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause.

questions.explanation: The best way to tackle cause and effect questions is to try to merge the two sectences into one using the format: "sentence 1...As a result,...sentence 2. If the new sentence makes sense, then sentence one is the cause and sentence 2 is the effect. So in this case, we could rewrite the two sentences as:"Ms. Kulsoom labelled Mr. Waleed a deserter. As a result, Ms. Kulsoom offered an apology." This makes sense. She did something wrong and as a result she is apologising. Hence we can say that sentence 1 is the cause, and sentence 2 is the effect.

questions.text: I. Since Amna’s baby died at birth, she wanted another immediately.II. Amna abducted an infant and took it out of the hospital.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
Both statements I and II are independent causes
Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause.

questions.explanation: The best way to tackle cause and effect questions is to try to merge the two sectences into one using the format: "sentence 1...As a result,...sentence 2. If the new sentence makes sense, then sentence one is the cause and sentence 2 is the effect. So in this case, we could rewrite the two sentences as: "Since Amna’s baby died at birth, she wanted another immediately. As a result, Amna abducted an infant and took it out of the hospital." This makes sense. Amna's own child died and as a result she stole another from the hospital. Hence we can say that sentence 1 is the cause, and sentence 2 is the effect.

questions.explanation: The best way to tackle cause and effect questions is to try to merge the two sentences into one using the format: "sentence 2…As a result,...sentence 1. If the new sentence makes sense, then sentence 2 is the cause and sentence 1 is the effect. So in this case, we could rewrite the two sentences as: The ministers of Muslim League and Indian National Congress will work on a plan to improvise the relations ... As a result,... Jinnah and Gandhi tried to take steps to improve the relations between both countries.

questions.text: STATEMENTS: I. The dialogue between Jinnah and Gandhi failed. II. Indian National Congress and Muslim League tried to join hands, so they will be united.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
Both statements I and II are independent causes
Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause.
options.explanation: The two statements provided are unrelated, and there is no clear cause-and-effect relationship between them based on the information provided. Statement I: "The dialogue between Jinnah and Gandhi failed." This statement states that the dialogue between two prominent leaders, Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Mahatma Gandhi, did not lead to a successful resolution or agreement. However, it does not provide any information about the context, reasons, or consequences of the failed dialogue. Statement II: "Indian National Congress and Muslim League tried to join hands, so they will be united." This statement suggests that the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League attempted to collaborate or work together, indicating an effort towards unity. However, it does not specify the extent of their collaboration or the outcome of their attempts to unite. Without additional information or context, we cannot establish any direct relationship between the failure of the dialogue between Jinnah and Gandhi (Statement I) and the attempted collaboration between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League (Statement II). The two statements may represent independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

questions.explanation: Gandhi and Jinnah were unable to reach a decision regarding Pakistan hence the dialogue failed. In order to show a united front to the British as they believe that would help them achieve independence INC and ML tried to join hands (work together). Thus both these statements are effects of independent causes.

questions.text: Statements: I. There is an unprecedented increase in the number of young unemployed in comparison to the previous year.II. A large number of candidates submitted applications against an advertisement for the post of manager issued by a bank.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it suggests that the increase in the number of young unemployed individuals (Statement I) is the cause, and the large number of job applications for the bank manager position (Statement II) is the effect. However, there is no logical or causal relationship between the two statements. They describe separate situations without any indication that one is causing the other.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it suggests that both the increase in the number of young unemployed individuals (Statement I) and the large number of job applications for the bank manager position (Statement II) are independent causes of something else. However, there is no context or information provided in the given statements to infer any relationship between these two scenarios or any external effect they might cause.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements I and II are not the effects of independent causes because each statement describes different situations with their separate underlying factors or causes.

questions.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

options.text: Both of the statements are independent.
options.explanation: Based on the given statements: Statement I: The children are being aggressive today. Statement II: There is no specific limit for children to use mobile screens. It is not clear that either statement is the cause and the other is the effect. Also, there is no evidence provided in the statements to establish a clear cause-and-effect relationship between them. Therefore, we cannot determine a direct cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements. Additionally, both statements appear to be independent of each other, as there is no apparent link or logical connection between children being aggressive and the absence of a specific limit for children to use mobile screens. The given information does not support any specific cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements or the existence of a common cause affecting both of them. Therefore, the correct answer is: Both of the statements are independent.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and I is the effect.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the given statement is not an example of cause and effect.

options.text: Both statements are the effects of some common cause.

questions.text: Statement I: Rural and semi-urban areas in the country have been suffering due to load shedding for quite some time. Statement II: If the government is not able to overcome the power crisis, load shedding will be extended even to the urban areas.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: a) Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect:This option suggests that Statement I is the reason or cause, and Statement II is the consequence or effect of that cause. Looking at the statements, we can see that Statement I describes the ongoing load shedding issue in rural and semi-urban areas, which implies that these areas are already experiencing power cuts. On the other hand, Statement II presents a possible future scenario where load shedding might be extended to urban areas if the government is unable to resolve the power crisis. So, Statement I is not a direct cause of Statement II.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect:This option suggests that Statement II is the reason or cause, and Statement I is the consequence or effect of that cause. However, the statements do not support this relationship. Statement I describes the current situation of load shedding in rural and semi-urban areas, and there is no indication that it is a direct effect of Statement II.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both statements I and II are independent causes:This option suggests that both statements are independent causes, meaning they are separate and unrelated reasons behind the power crisis and load shedding situation. This could be a plausible interpretation as both statements describe different aspects of the power crisis issue. Statement I talks about the suffering of rural and semi-urban areas due to load shedding, and Statement II discusses the potential extension of load shedding to urban areas. There may be multiple factors contributing to the power crisis, and these statements could highlight different facets of the problem.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: d) Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause:This option suggests that both statements are consequences or effects of a shared or common underlying cause. In this context, the common cause could be the power crisis itself, leading to load shedding in both rural and semi-urban areas (Statement I) and the potential extension of load shedding to urban areas (Statement II). The power crisis may be the root issue responsible for the situations described in both statements.

questions.explanation: d) Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause:This option suggests that both statements are consequences or effects of a shared or common underlying cause. In this context, the common cause could be the power crisis itself, leading to load shedding in both rural and semi-urban areas (Statement I) and the potential extension of load shedding to urban areas (Statement II). The power crisis may be the root issue responsible for the situations described in both statements.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes. Statement: There has been a high increase in the incidents of atrocities against women in the city during the past few months. The police authority has been unable to nab theculprits who are committing crime against women.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is wrong because statement I is not the cause and statement II is not its effects. Both statements are independent causes.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is wrong because statement II is not cause and statement I is not its effect. Statement I and Statement II are independent causes.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements are independent causes as there is no link between cases of atrocities on women and police being unable to nab the culprits.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is wrong because both statements are not effects of independent causes. They are independent causes.

questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because statement I is not cause and statement II is not its effects. Both statements are independent causes. Option B is wrong because statement II is not cause and statement I is not its effect. Statement I and Statement II are independent causes. Option: C Both statements are independent causes as there is no link between cases of atrocities on women and police being unable to nab the culprits. Option D is wrong because both statements are not effects of independent causes. They are independent causes.

questions.text: Statement: There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year. The government has decided to increase the import quantum of edible oil.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Option A is correct because , there is sharp decline in production of oilseed this year and because of this the government has decided to increase import quantum of edible oil. As option A is the only correct option so all other options are incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect (Option b):Option (b) suggests that the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil (Statement II) is the cause, and the sharp decline in oilseed production (Statement I) is the effect. This option implies that the government's decision to increase imports somehow resulted in the decline in oilseed production.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both the statements I and II are independent causes (Option c):Option (c) suggests that both the decline in the production of oilseeds and the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil are independent causes and not directly related to each other.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: d) Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause (Option d):Option (d) suggests that both the decline in the production of oilseeds and the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil are effects of some common cause, meaning there is an underlying reason or factor that influenced both situations.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct because , there is sharp decline in production of oilseed this year and because of this the government has decided to increase import quantum of edible oil. As option A is the only correct option so all other options are incorrect.

questions.text: Verbal Reasoning Statements (I) She topped her collage. (II) She is hard-working.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Both statements have some common cause.
options.explanation: states that both statements have some common cause. However, there is no direct evidence to suggest a common cause behind the two statements. Being hardworking may be a factor contributing to topping in college, but it does not establish a common cause between the two statements. Therefore, option A is incorrect.

options.text: Statement 1 is the cause, and Statement 2 is its effect.
options.explanation: claims that statement 1 is the cause and statement 2 is its effect. However, the order of cause and effect is reversed. It is more reasonable to assume that being hardworking (statement 2) leads to topping in college (statement 1) rather than the other way around. Therefore, option B is incorrect.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause, and Statement 1 is its effect.
options.explanation: suggests that statement 2 is the cause and statement 1 is the effect. This option aligns with our understanding that being hardworking (statement 2) is a characteristic or quality that can lead to academic success and topping in college (statement 1). Therefore, option C is correct.

options.text: Both of the statements are the effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: proposes that both statements are the effects of independent causes. This option implies that there are separate, unrelated causes for both statements. However, based on the context provided, it is more reasonable to interpret statement 2 (being hardworking) as the cause and statement 1 (topping in college) as its effect. Therefore, option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The reason she topped her college was that she’s hardworking, which tells us that she studied.

questions.text: Verbal Reasoning Statements : A Large number of Primary Schools in rural areas are run by only one teacher. II. There has been a huge dropout rate from primary schools in rural areas.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement 1 is the cause, and statement 2 is its effect.
options.explanation: When analyzing the relationship between these two statements, we can see that Statement 1 is the cause and Statement 2 is the effect. Here's the reasoning: In many rural areas, due to limited resources and infrastructure, a large number of primary schools operate with only one teacher. This means that a single teacher is responsible for teaching multiple grades or subjects. As a result, the quality of education may suffer, and the teacher may struggle to provide individual attention to students. This situation can lead to an inadequate learning environment, which may discourage students and contribute to a lack of interest in attending school.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause, and Statement 1 is its effect.
options.explanation: Statement 2 is the cause and Statement 1 is its effect, which is incorrect because the given statements do not establish a causal relationship between the two. The statements describe two separate conditions in primary schools in rural areas, but they do not indicate that one statement directly causes the other. Therefore, option B is not correct.

options.text: Both statements have independent causes.
options.explanation: Both statements are effects of independent causes, which is also incorrect. The statements themselves do not provide any information about the causes of the conditions mentioned. While it is possible that both statements have independent causes, the given information does not support this conclusion. Without additional evidence or context, it is not appropriate to assume independent causes for the conditions described. Therefore, option C is not correct.

options.text: Both of the statements are the effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: D is incorrect because it combines incorrect options B and C. As explained above, option B and option C are both incorrect, so option D, which includes both of them, is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: One teacher running a lot of schools leads to mismanagement and lowers the educational standard, which causes students to drop out of school in rural areas.

However, there is no apparent causal relationship between these two statements.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes

However, the statements seem to be related, as there might be a potential connection between the increase in unemployment and the number of applications submitted for the bank manager position.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes:I. The student was expelled from the school. II. He was found using the drugs.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: a) Statement I is the cause, and Statement II is its effect.This option suggests that Statement I (The student was expelled from the school) is the cause, and Statement II (He was found using drugs) is the effect of that cause. In other words, the student's expulsion from school led to the discovery that he was using drugs.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause, and Statement I is its effect.This option suggests the opposite of option a), stating that Statement II (He was found using drugs) is the cause, and Statement I (The student was expelled from the school) is the effect. In this interpretation, the discovery of drug use resulted in the student's expulsion from school.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both statements I and II are independent causes.This option suggests that both events (the student's expulsion and the discovery of drug use) are independent of each other, meaning they are not related as cause and effect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.
options.explanation: d) Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.This option implies that there is another independent cause that led to both events happening. In this case, the expulsion and the discovery of drug use are outcomes resulting from a common cause.

questions.explanation: the school probably has a no drug policy so when the school found the person with drugs they expelled him.

questions.text: Dependent Causes/ Independent Causes: Statement: All the schools in the area had to be kept closed for most of the week. Many parents have withdrawn their children from the local schools.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is no clear cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements. The closure of schools (Statement I) doesn't directly lead to parents withdrawing their children (Statement II).

options.text: Statement II is the cause, and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is also incorrect because there is no evidence to suggest that parents withdrawing their children (Statement II) is causing the closure of schools (Statement I). If anything, it would be the other way around—if the schools were closed, some parents might choose to withdraw their children, but again, it's not a direct cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements are describing independent causes that are not directly linked to each other. The closure of schools (Statement I) could be due to reasons such as extreme weather conditions, a health-related emergency, or a labor strike by teachers, among other possibilities.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes. This option is correct, and it summarizes the relationship between the two statements accurately. Both events seem to be the effects of different independent causes, rather than one causing the other.

questions.explanation: "Closing the schools for a week and the parents withdrawing their wards from the local schools are independent issues", which must have been triggered by different individual causes.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes:Statement: The performance of most of the students in the final exam of class X in the schools run by the government was excellent. Many teachers of government schools left the school and joined private schools.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: a) Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.This option suggests that statement I (excellent performance of students in class X final exam) is the cause, and statement II (teachers leaving government schools and joining private schools) is its effect. According to this interpretation, the excellent performance of students led to teachers leaving government schools. However, there is no direct causal relationship established between the two statements in the given information. So, this option is not correct.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.This option suggests that statement II (teachers leaving government schools and joining private schools) is the cause, and statement I (excellent performance of students in class X final exam) is its effect. According to this interpretation, teachers leaving government schools caused the students' excellent performance. However, the given information does not indicate a direct causal link between the two statements. So, this option is not correct.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both the statements I and II are independent causes.This option suggests that both statement I (excellent performance of students) and statement II (teachers leaving government schools) are independent causes, meaning they are not directly related to each other causally. According to this interpretation, there might be other factors contributing to both the excellent performance of students and teachers leaving government schools. This option is plausible based on the given information.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: The students of government schools performing well in the examinations and the teachers of government schools leaving their jobs to join private schools are two separate situations that must have been triggered by independent causes.

questions.explanation: The students of government schools performing well in the examinations and the teachers of government schools leaving their jobs to join private schools are two separate situations that must have been triggered by independent causes.

questions.text: Dependent causes / independent causes:I. The selection team has decided to keep the same players for the semi-final and final match of the series against Australia. II. England lost against Australia.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: a) Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.Explanation: This option suggests that the selection team's decision to retain players (Statement I) is the cause, and England's loss (Statement II) is the effect. However, there is no direct cause-and-effect relationship established between these two statements in the given context. The selection team's decision and England's loss are independent events, and one does not directly lead to the other.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.Explanation: This option suggests that England's loss (Statement II) is the cause, and the selection team's decision to retain players (Statement I) is its effect. Again, there is no direct cause-and-effect relationship between these two statements in the given context. England's loss and the selection team's decision are independent events.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both statements I and II are independent causes.Explanation: This option correctly identifies that both statements I and II are independent causes. There is no direct cause-and-effect relationship between the selection team's decision and England's loss. They are separate events that occur independently.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: d) Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.Explanation: This option suggests that both statements I and II are effects of a common independent cause. However, the statements do not provide any information about a common independent cause that leads to both the selection team's decision and England's loss. Therefore, this option is not supported by the given statements.

questions.explanation: We cannot blame England losing against Australia due to no changes in the team, hence both these statements are independent causes.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes: Statement: I:- There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year. II:- The government has decided to increase the import of edible oil.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement 1 is the cause and statement 2 is the effect. The sharp decline in the production of oilseeds has led the government to increase the import quantum of edible oil.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: There is an underlying factor or reason that has caused both the decline in oilseeds production and the government's decision to increase edible oil imports. For example, it could be due to unfavorable weather conditions, changes in agricultural policies, or economic factors that affect both the domestic production of oilseeds and the decision to import more edible oil.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This is incorrect because statement II (government's decision to increase import) is the cause for the effect stated in statement I (decline in oilseed production).

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: This is incorrect because there is no indication of a common cause leading to both the decline in oilseed production and the government's decision to increase import. The relationship between the two statements is one of cause and effect, with statement II causing statement I.

questions.explanation: Statement 1 is the cause and statement 2 is the effect. The sharp decline in production of oilseeds has led the government to increase the import quantum of edible oil.

questions.text: Dependent/Independent causes: I. There are orders by the high court to clean Karachi. II. More than 500 people died in Karachi due to land pollution.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the cause-effect relationship is the opposite of what is stated in option (a). As explained above, Statement II (the high number of deaths due to land pollution) is the cause, and Statement I (orders by the high court to clean Karachi) is its effect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Based on the given statements: I. There are orders by the high court to clean Karachi. II. More than 500 people died in Karachi due to land pollution. The relationship between the statements can be analyzed as follows: Statement I implies that there is an order from the high court to clean Karachi. This suggests that there is a recognition of a problem and an attempt to address it. The order to clean Karachi can be seen as a response to an existing issue or problem. Statement II indicates that more than 500 people died in Karachi due to land pollution. This statement presents the consequence or effect of the land pollution issue in Karachi, resulting in loss of life. Based on this analysis, we can conclude that Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect. The pollution and resulting deaths (Statement II) likely prompted the high court to issue orders for cleaning Karachi (Statement I). Therefore, the correct answer is: B) Statement II is the cause, and statement I is its effect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is a cause-effect relationship between the two statements. Statement I (orders by the high court) is a response to the situation described in Statement II (deaths due to land pollution). The two statements are not independent causes.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is a cause-effect relationship between the two statements, as explained above. The high court's orders (Statement I) are in response to the problem of land pollution and the resulting deaths (Statement II). The two statements are not independent effects.

questions.explanation: Based on the given statements: I. There are orders by the high court to clean Karachi. II. More than 500 people died in Karachi due to land pollution. The relationship between the statements can be analyzed as follows: Statement I implies that there is an order from the high court to clean Karachi. This suggests that there is a recognition of a problem and an attempt to address it. The order to clean Karachi can be seen as a response to an existing issue or problem. Statement II indicates that more than 500 people died in Karachi due to land pollution. This statement presents the consequence or effect of the land pollution issue in Karachi, resulting in loss of life. Based on this analysis, we can conclude that Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect. The pollution and resulting deaths (Statement II) likely prompted the high court to issue orders for cleaning Karachi (Statement I). Therefore, the correct answer is: B) Statement II is the cause, and statement I is its effect.

questions.text: Dependent/Independent causes: Statement I: The school authority has asked the FSc students to attend special classes to be conducted on Sundays. Statement II: The parents of the FSc students have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions conducted on Sundays.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: It seems quite evident that the parents have instructed their wards to abstain from private tuitions on Sundays and attend special classes organized by the school.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: It seems quite evident that the parents have instructed their wards to abstain from private tuitions on Sundays and attend special classes organized by the school.

questions.text: Dependent/Independent causes: I. The queen expressed her desire to marry the king again. II. The king would not remarry his ex-wife.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The king's decision not to remarry his ex-wife, stated in Statement II, acts as the cause. Consequently, the effect described in Statement I occurs, as the queen expresses her desire to marry the king again in response to his decision.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The king's decision not to remarry his ex-wife, stated in Statement II, acts as the cause. Consequently, the effect described in Statement I occurs, as the queen expresses her desire to marry the king again in response to his decision.

questions.text: Dependent/Independent causes: I. The central bureau of investigation has called Harry and Sam for interrogation. II. Harry and Sam have been alleged for match-fixing.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Based on the provided statements, it appears that statement II is the cause and statement I is the effect. Statement II states that Harry and Sam have been alleged of match-fixing. This suggests that there are suspicions or accusations surrounding their involvement in match-fixing, which serves as the cause. Statement I states that the central bureau of Investigation has called Harry and Sam for interrogation. This suggests that the investigative bureau is taking action in response to the allegations of match-fixing, making it the effect of the alleged match-fixing.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Based on the provided statements, it appears that statement II is the cause and statement I is the effect. Statement II states that Harry and Sam have been alleged of match-fixing. This suggests that there are suspicions or accusations surrounding their involvement in match-fixing, which serves as the cause. Statement I states that the central bureau of Investigation has called Harry and Sam for interrogation. This suggests that the investigative bureau is taking action in response to the allegations of match-fixing, making it the effect of the alleged match-fixing.

questions.text: Statement I: There is a considerable reduction in the number of people affected by water-borne diseases in the city during this rainy season. Statement II: The government opened four new civil hospitals in City A at the beginning of the year.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option implies that the considerable reduction in water-borne diseases is a consequence of the government opening four new civil hospitals. In other words, the improved healthcare facilities provided by the new hospitals are believed to have led to the reduction in water-borne diseases. However, both events are independent of each other.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option suggests that the government's decision to open new civil hospitals was a response to the considerable reduction in water-borne diseases. In other words, the decreased incidence of water-borne diseases prompted the government to take action and open new hospitals. However, both events are independent of each other.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option implies that the reduction in water-borne diseases and the opening of new hospitals are both effects of separate and independent causes. These events are not causally connected to each other. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option implies that the reduction in water-borne diseases and the opening of new hospitals are both effects of separate and independent causes. These events are not causally connected to each other. Hence, it is correct option.

questions.explanation: Water-borne diseases are not linked with no hospitals hence opening new hospitals does not reduce it. Thus, both statements are effects of independent causes.

questions.text: I. There has been a decline in the prices of personal computers. II. School-goings are expressing interest in learning how the computer operates.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Option I suggests that the decline in the prices of personal computers (Statement I) is the cause of school-goers expressing interest in learning how the computer operates (Statement II). It is an incorrect option.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Option II proposes that school-goers expressing interest in learning how the computer operates (Statement II) is the cause of the decline in the prices of personal computers (Statement I). It is an incorrect option.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: It is an incorrect option.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: Because the use of computers for personal use has died over the years, and maybe due to that there has been a decline in their prices. The second statement states that students are interested in the operation of computers due to xyz reasons and it is in no way linked to the first statement.

questions.explanation: Because the use of computers for personal use has died over the years, and maybe due to that there has been a decline in their prices. The second statement states that students are interested in the operation of computers due to xyz reasons and it is in no way linked to the first statement.

questions.text: Statement (A) The Government has imported large quantities of sugar as per trade agreements with other countries. Statement (B) The prices of sugar in the domestic market have fallen sharply in recent months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement A is the cause and statement B Is its effect.
options.explanation: Due to import of sugar (cause) Prices are decreased (effect).

options.text: Statement B is the cause and statement A is its effect
options.explanation: Decrease in prices is not causing import of sugar …. It will be totally opposite.

options.text: Both statements are independent causes.
options.explanation: As discussed earlier, this can never be an option.

options.text: Both statements are effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: They are dependent on each other.

questions.explanation: In logical reasoning, there are questions that come under the topic called cause and effect. In these question, they give us two statements, we have to relate those statementsand sometimes they do have no relation(In this case , we will select option D, Both statements are effects of independent causes.Option is C ( Both statements are independent causes) is never the right option, because causes don't exist without effect.In this question they have given two statements and they do relate because when the government has imported large quantities of sugar, there is a bulk of sugaravailable in the domestic market and the price will be low. This is the rule of economics that whenever something is in bulk, the price gets low and wheneversomething is scarce, the price goes high.So the first statement is cause due to which an effect is seen that is statement B

questions.text: Statements: I. More than two billion pounds of chemicals are spewed in the air everyday by industries and vehicles of some countries. II. International pollution control organisation is expected to penalise the countries which cross the maximum emission limits.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
Both the statement I and II are independent causes.
Both the statement I and II are effects of independent causes.
Both the statement I and II are effects of some common causes.

questions.explanation: Statement I is the cause as it refers to the amount of pollution released by industries and vehicles in the air every day. Statement II is its effect as International pollution control impose penalty on the countries which cross the maximum emission limits.

questions.text: Statements: The Income Tax authorities carried out raids at three different business houses in the city last week. Which of the following can be a possible effect of the above statement?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: The three business houses are regular defaulters in payment of their income tax.
The Income Tax department had received a tip off about the illegal activities going on in the three business houses.
The Government decided to look into the matter and has appointed an inquiry committee.
Other business houses took immediate action to clear off all their income tax dues in order to avoid a raid on their establishments.
None of these

questions.explanation: It is clear that option D is the effect of the given statement.
